items.title: Automatic EPF transfer after changing jobs: Things to check before your balance is moved to the new account
items.summary: EPFO has introduced automatic PF transfers for eligible members, simplifying the job-switch process. Here are the key things to check before your balance is moved to the new account.

items.title: Attention taxpayers! Here's what you should do after receiving a notice from the I-T department — Stepwise guide
items.summary: Income-tax returns: Today, we take a look at what taxpayers should do if they receive a notice from the I-T department. Individuals can use this stepwise guide to ensure they are following the proper process.

items.title: Facebook, Instagram face outage as users report access issues
items.summary: According to Downdetector, which tracks outages by aggregating user-submitted problem reports, the complaints peaked around the afternoon and eased after a couple of hours. Users reported problems in logging into their accounts in several parts of the globe, including India.

items.summary: Although the economic calendar is relatively subdued, several key reports could still influence market sentiment

items.title: Can forming a HUF help you save tax? Experts explain who can create one, key benefits and what to consider
items.summary: Forming a Hindu Undivided Family (HUF) can help taxpayers manage taxes efficiently as it is a separate taxable entity under income tax law, however a family must understand how it works first. Here's what experts say.

items.title: Gifting to your NRI child? Know the tax treatment of NRE deposits
items.summary: Gifts from parents to NRI children are tax-free, while interest earned on NRE accounts and deposits is fully exempt from income tax for FEMA non-residents, unlike taxable NRO interest.

items.title: EPFO: Here's how to update your KYC information online using the official portal — Stepwise guide
items.summary: EPFO: Aadhaar linking and KYC updating is mandatory to avail several EPF services. Today, we check how EPF subscribers can update their KYC information online for their account using the retirement body's official portal.

items.title: India, Spain to fast-track UPI-Bizum interoperability talks
items.summary: The two countries have also discussed ways to strengthen investment facilitation, improving mobility of professionals and promoting joint investments in Latin America, leveraging Spain's strategic position as India's gateway to Europe and Latin America.

items.title: Want to build wealth through property? First read these 2 ‘golden rules’ from a financially independent millennial
items.summary: Cody Berman, author of 'Retire by 30', used house hacking to achieve financial independence by investing in real estate. By renting part of his home, he reduced his housing costs and generated passive income.

items.title: Who's really making money from the AI boom? Follow the cash behind OpenAI, Nvidia & Big Tech
items.summary: The AI boom has triggered massive spending, with companies investing heavily in infrastructure, startups, and data centers. However, uncertainties persist regarding the flow of investments and the industry’s sustainability, particularly if major players like OpenAI were to fail.

items.title: Reliance Retail plans to expand online business and dark stores in FY27, CFO Dinesh Taluja lays out three-year roadmap
items.summary: Reliance Retail plans to ‘rapidly’ scale its online business, expand dark stores and strengthen omni-channel capabilities, CFO Dinesh Taluja told PTI. The company plans to evaluate markets and focus investments accordingly, he added.&nbsp;

items.title: Piramal Finance bets on in-house AI to cut costs, boost efficiency
items.summary: Piramal Finance is trying to use AI to save money, not to spend even more.

items.title: ASEAN, Africa lead India's export growth in first two months of FY27
items.summary: ASEAN and Africa were the biggest drivers of India's export growth in the first two months of FY27, with exports to ASEAN surging 66.9% to $10.5 billion and shipments to Africa rising 53.1% to $9.6 billion, according to commerce ministry data. Together, the two regions contributed over $7.6 billion in additional exports during April-May.

items.title: ITR filing 2026: Secondary address field is now mandatory — here's how it can help taxpayers
items.summary: The income tax department has mandated a secondary address field in all ITR forms for AY 2026-27, requiring additional communication details from taxpayers. Here's how it can help taxpayers.&nbsp;

items.summary: Ahead of its planned IPO, iD Fresh is targeting best-in-class profitability, expanding into snacks and scaling new product launches as quick commerce reshapes India's ready-to-cook foods market.

items.title: DA hike: Here's the full list of states that are considering Dearness Allowance increases for employees, pensioners

items.title: Don't miss this tax benefit: How car buyers can claim a minimum  ₹10,000 refund
items.summary: Car buyers may be eligible for an income tax refund due to 1% TCS collected on vehicle purchases exceeding  ₹10 lakh. This amount can be lower you tax liability or get you a refund if their tax payable is less than the TCS paid. Details here.

items.title: Government launches White Rabbit tech network to secure Indian Standard Time
items.summary: The system disseminates Indian Standard Time, traceable to the national time-keeping authority UTC (NPLI), using Precision Time Protocol-based White Rabbit technology, which the ministry said in a statement, offers highly accurate synchronisation for sectors, including banking, telecommunications, power grids, transportation, and digital governance.

items.title: Amul flaunts Argentina and Spain sponsorships in World Cup final print ad
items.summary:
As the FIFA World Cup reached its final act, Amul has taken out a full-page advertisement in The Times of India to celebrate a position few brands can claim.

The dairy major is an official regional sponsor of both Argentina and Spain, the two teams contestin

items.title: TDS Return Filing 2026: Q1 Due on July 31, Check New Forms and Section Codes
items.summary: Businesses and tax deductors must file Q1 TDS and TCS returns by July 31, 2026 using the new return forms and revised section codes under the Income-tax Act, 2025. Here's what has changed, the new forms to use, and the compliance steps to avoid filing errors.

items.title: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan, Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi hold "wide-ranging, productive discussions" in bilateral meeting
items.summary: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan met Zanzibar President Hussein Ali Mwinyi in New Delhi.  Discussions focused on strengthening trade, investment, and development partnerships between the nations.  Zanzibar's President praised the new IIT Madras campus as a landmark educational initiative.  External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar also met President Mwinyi to deepen bilateral ties.  The visit highlights growing momentum in the India-Tanzania strategic partnership.

items.title: The F&O trap inside the barracks: How army officers are losing crores chasing the next trade
items.summary: Stable salaries, easy access to credit and early trading wins are drawing serving and retired defence personnel into a cycle of leveraged bets—and mounting losses

items.title: Samsung layoffs: Over 800 US workers affected by Texas headquarters move
items.summary: Samsung layoffs: 739 positions at Samsung Electronics America in Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, were impacted as part of the company's plan to relocate its U.S. consumer electronics headquarters to Texas

items.title: Can builder's loose papers lead to an income tax notice? ITAT explains the law
items.summary: A Mumbai ITAT ruling has held that the Income Tax Department cannot tax a property buyer merely because their name appears in a builder's seized cash records. The Revenue must prove an undisclosed cash payment with independent corroborative evidence before invoking Section 69A.

items.title: ITR filing 2026: When should a salaried taxpayer file ITR-2 instead of ITR-1?
items.summary: Taxpayers must file accurate income tax returns using the correct ITR form to avoid being flagged for defects. The e-filing portal aids in selection. Here's when a salaried taxpayer needs to file ITR-2 instead of ITR-1.&nbsp;

items.title: AMFI's Mutual Funds Sahi Hai shares a list of dos and don'ts for investors to avoid online frauds
items.summary: AMFI’s investor education initiative “Mutual Funds Sahi Hai” advised investors not to share sensitive information such as OTPs, passwords, and investment details with unauthorised distributors.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide D-Street action on Monday
items.summary: The Sensex surged over 960 points and the Nifty climbed more than 1% on Friday, driven by strong Q1 earnings optimism and buying in IT and banking stocks. Analysts said domestic institutional investors are rotating into attractively valued large-cap stocks, while selective profit booking in mid- and small-caps continues. Consumer durables also remain in focus on improving demand expectations.

items.title: Nifty breakout isn’t convincing yet; a move above 24,600 could unlock record high: Anand James
items.summary: Nifty’s Friday rebound has broken its recent range, but a sustained uptrend is yet to be confirmed. Geojit’s Anand James sees 24,400 as near-term resistance and flags India Cements and Bharat Forge as top trading picks.

items.title: Accenture reworks salary hikes to include more employees: Splits base pay and one-time lumpsum — Here's what changes
items.summary: Accenture has reworked its salary hike composition to include more employees in the June cycle, according to a report. The new approach aims to reward more staff while managing payroll. Here's a look at what changes…&nbsp;

items.title: 5paisa Capital partners with OpenAI to bring AI-powered investing in India
items.summary: 5paisa Capital has partnered with OpenAI to integrate AI-powered investing and trading features across its platform. The collaboration will offer personalised insights, portfolio analysis and decision support, with plans to make 5paisa's services accessible within ChatGPT through conversational AI.

items.summary: Early Q1 results have also helped reinforce expectations that Indian equities may outperform global peers over the medium term, aided by resilient domestic demand and more reasonable valuations

items.summary: The market valuation of five top firms increased by  ₹1.54 lakh crore last week, led by TCS, amid positive equity trends. The Sensex and Nifty also rose, supported by strong IT earnings and renewed interest in financial stocks.&nbsp;

items.summary: A US judge has ruled that Meta can proceed with layoffs impacting 8,000 employees, despite disputes over AI usage in selecting job cuts.&nbsp;

items.summary: On average, active equity mutual funds have generated higher annualised returns while limiting losses better than their benchmark indices. An average active small-cap fund posted the strongest outperformance among the funds have been live since 2013.

items.title: Nifty weekly outlook: 24,500 holds the key to next leg of gains; focus on stock-specific bets
items.summary: The Nifty ended the week with modest gains as the index defended the crucial 23,800–24,000 support zone, keeping the broader technical structure positive. Analysts expect the next move to hinge on the 24,500 resistance level, where the 100-week moving average could pose a hurdle. A breakout above this zone may revive stronger upside momentum, while support remains intact.

items.title: Dr Reddy's Foundation soil testing lab set to hit 1-lakh sample mark ahead of 2026 rabi season
items.summary: Dr Reddy's Foundation is expanding its soil testing operations significantly.  The Hyderabad lab aims to process one lakh samples before the next Rabi season.  This initiative addresses concerns about depleting soil health and government scheme implementation gaps.  The foundation also partners with a startup for biological soil amendments.  Collaboration with ICAR-IISS will build a digital soil health repository and advanced advisories.

items.title: Late-night Argentina-Spain Fifa final fuels business for bars, hotels and delivery apps
items.summary: Extended bar timings, packed screenings and a surge in food delivery orders are turning the Fifa World Cup final into an unexpected late-night revenue opportunity for India's hospitality industry.

items.title: Users of Meta's Facebook, Instagram report suffering some outages
items.summary: Social media platforms Instagram and Facebook experienced widespread outages on Sunday. Users in the United States reported significant issues accessing both the app and website. Downdetector data showed thousands of reports filed by affected users. Access to these popular platforms was also intermittent in Singapore. Meta has not yet provided an immediate comment on the disruptions.

items.title: Buy & hold? Think again: James ‘RevShark’ DePorre says invest like a shark, not a spectator
items.summary: Veteran strategist James 'RevShark' DePorre challenges the passive "buy and hold" mantra, arguing investors should stay alert and react to market conditions rather than predict them. Capital preservation and disciplined risk management form the backbone of his five-point investing philosophy.

items.title: Zee 5 unveils its biggest multilingual slate yet across seven languages
items.summary: Zee 5 has unveiled its most ambitious multilingual slate yet, a bold showcase of stories, creators, and formats designed for a new generation of audiences. items.title: Afghanistan seeks deeper trade ties with India, pitches for easier business access
items.summary: Afghanistan's trade body seeks enhanced economic ties with India.  They requested easier business procedures and faster visa issuance.  This comes as Afghanistan diversifies trade routes away from Pakistan.  India is strengthening connectivity through Iran's Chabahar port.  Both nations aim for sustainable cooperation and strengthened relations.

items.title: NITI Aayog to review industrial corridor framework, seeks reforms to speed project execution
items.summary: India's government is reviewing industrial corridor frameworks to enhance project delivery. NITI Aayog will consult stakeholders on necessary policy and regulatory changes. The review aims to identify bottlenecks and recommend reforms for industrial competitiveness. This initiative supports the nation's goal of becoming a developed country by 2047.

items.title: Jio says Arpu is not under pressure. Analysts aren't so sure
items.summary: Jio says organic Arpu growth is still running at 4-5% without tariff hikes. But analysts warn the easy gains may be over, forcing telcos to look beyond connectivity for growth.

items.summary: According to Seema Srivastava, Senior Research Analyst at SMC Global Securities, for long-term investors, the private lender is the defensive compounder.

items.title: ITR Filing AY 2026-27: Don't miss any bank account while filing your tax return. Here's why
items.summary: The ITR forms for AY 2026-27 require taxpayers to disclose all Indian bank accounts held during FY 2025-26, except officially dormant accounts. Accurate bank details are also essential for timely income tax refunds.

items.title: Deloitte projects 6.5-6.8% GDP expansion in FY27, growth to strengthen in second half
items.summary: Deloitte India on Sunday projected India's economy to grow between 6.5% and 6.8% in the current financial year, with momentum expected to pick up in the second half of FY27. The firm said growth would be supported by stronger festive demand, monetary easing and a gradual stabilisation in global economic conditions.

items.title: JSW Steel sees West Asia reconstruction opportunity, warns of steel import diversion
items.summary: JSW Steel expects post-war reconstruction in West Asia to create fresh demand, while warning that surplus steel from China, Japan and Russia could be diverted to India.

items.title: Looking to invest in debt funds for a 1-3 year horizon? Expert shares 4 categories you can consider
items.summary: For a 1-3 year investment horizon, short-duration, dynamic bond, corporate bond, and banking &amp; PSU funds are among the best debt fund categories you can consider. Unlike others, credit risk funds invest in lower-rated bonds, making them riskier.

items.title: Should you buy Reliance shares after strong Q1 results 2026?

Pocket-size AI: Powerful phones star at China show
items.summary: Tech firms are developing AI agent smartphones that could revolutionize mobile use. These advanced phones aim to perform tasks using voice commands and AI. Major app companies are restricting access, which hinders the AI agent's functionality. Companies like Nubia, Honor, and StepFun are showcasing these new devices. Experts predict a fundamental change in digital economies and business models.

items.title: Bank seized your property after loan default? RBI's new rules explained
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has issued a new framework governing how banks must value, manage and sell properties acquired while recovering defaulted loans. The rules, effective October 1, 2026, also bar banks from selling such properties back to defaulting borrowers.

items.title: Q1 results 2026: Infosys to Eternal among companies to declare Q1 results next week; check full list here
items.summary: Q1 results 2026: Infosys, Eternal, One 97 Communications Paytm, Bajaj Auto, Adani Energy Solutions, Indian Hotels Company, Bharat Petroleum Corporation, Interglobe Aviation, NTPC, and IDFC First Bank are among the marquee companies to report their Q1 results FY27 next week.

items.title: M-cap of five of top 10 most valued firms jumps Rs 1.54 lakh cr; TCS biggest winner
items.summary: Last week marked a significant uplift in the market valuation of five of the top 10 firms, appreciating by Rs 1.54 lakh crore. Tata Consultancy Services claimed the spotlight, boosting its valuation by Rs 72,072.3 crore. ICICI Bank and Reliance Industries also experienced notable increases. However, the tide turned for HDFC Bank and Bharti Airtel, which faced declines. Overall, Indian equity markets concluded the week on a robust note.

items.title: Government may roll out flexible pension scheme for all workers under EPFO 3.0 reforms: Report
items.summary: The proposed contributory pension scheme could cover formal, unorganised, gig and higher-wage workers, with flexible withdrawals and a “Target Retirement Sum” approach.

items.title: Axis Bank standalone profit soars 23% in Q1
items.summary: Axis Bank on Saturday reported a 23% year-on-year increase in standalone quarterly net profit, aided by lower provisions, even as its pre-provision operating profit grew just 1.3%.

items.title: Kotak Bank Q1 net rises 26% on loan growth
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank experienced a remarkable net profit surge of twenty-six percent in the June quarter. This impressive growth can be attributed to a significant decline in provisions that bolstered the bank's financial results. Additionally, net interest income rose by nine percent, while operating profit saw a ten percent increase.

items.title: Yes Bank Q1 profit surges 34% on strong loan growth
items.summary: Yes Bank has reported a remarkable thirty-four percent surge in net profit for the first quarter, driven by robust loan growth and enhancing net interest margins. The bank's loan portfolio expanded by eighteen percent, especially within corporate advances, while commercial banking loans rose by seventeen percent. The management is optimistic that this trend of positive loan growth will persist in the upcoming months.

items.title: Indian stock market: How are Sensex and Nifty 50 likely to perform next week amid global tech sell-off?
items.summary: Indian stock market: The benchmark indices ended the week on a strong note, with the Nifty 50 rising about 0.53% to close at 24,334.30, while the Sensex climbed nearly 0.75% to settle at 78,151.45

items.title: ICICI Bank net profit jumps 16% YoY to Rs 14,805 crore
items.summary: ICICI Bank reported a 15.9% year-on-year rise in Q1 FY27 net profit to Rs 14,805 crore, driven by healthy growth in net interest income, strong fee income and lower provisions. Net interest income increased 12.7% to Rs 24,384 crore, while fee income jumped 23.5%. The bank's net interest margin remained stable at 4.36% during the June quarter.

items.title: HDFC Bank logs 5% rise in profit on margin pressure
items.summary: HDFC Bank announced a notable five percent rise in profits for the June quarter, showcasing a robust growth in net interest income. Despite facing margin pressures that led to a contraction, the bank achieved a remarkable fifteen percent increase in gross advances, largely fueled by business and corporate loans. Additionally, deposits grew significantly, indicating strong customer engagement and successful operating efficiency strategies during the period.

items.title: Retirement planning: Why the rule of 1% upgrade works better than a 1% increase in SIP amount
items.summary: The rule of 1% upgrade encourages investors to increase the share of their income invested by one percentage point every year. A small annual increase in the share of income invested can significantly boost long-term retirement savings.

items.summary: “I was in denial of the idea of quick commerce. Back then, if you had asked 100 people whether they…

items.title: EPF Scheme 2026: Why you must keep 25% of your PF balance before retirement
items.summary: The new EPF Scheme introduces a 25% minimum balance requirement for partial withdrawals. Here's what the rule means, how eligible member balance is calculated, and when you can withdraw your full PF corpus.

items.title: Cube Highways Trust InvIT, Indo MIM among 4 public issues opening this week
items.summary: Next week will witness the launch of four public offerings, including the Cube Highways Trust InvIT and three initial public offerings (IPOs). Notably, Indo MIM's IPO comprises both a fresh issue and an offer for sale, while Lohia Corp's venture consists solely of equity shares available in the offer for sale format. Additionally, Xtranet Technologies is set to introduce a Rs 170-crore fresh issue aimed at financing various initiatives.

items.title: Equity norms under Semicon 2.0 to drive investment for advance chips design by Indian cos: IT Secy
items.summary: India's Semicon 2.0 program will offer equity incentives for chip design firms. This initiative aims to attract significant investment for advanced chip development. The government will co-invest with venture capitalists to fund high-end chip designs. India's focus is on deepening its chip design ecosystem and developing intellectual property.

items.title: Q1 earnings this week: Infosys, Eternal, Bajaj Auto among 256 companies set to announce June quarter results
items.summary: The Q1 FY27 earnings season gathers momentum this week, with 256 companies scheduled to announce their April-June quarter results. Key names include Infosys, Eternal, Bajaj Auto, UltraTech Cement, Nestlé India, Adani Power, Paytm and TVS Motor. Investors will closely track corporate earnings and management commentary for cues on business performance, demand trends and the broader market outlook.

items.title: ₹25,000 monthly SIP at 12%: Check estimated corpus after 10, 20 and 30 years and see how compounding builds  ₹8.74 crore
items.summary: A  ₹25,000 monthly SIP earning an assumed 12% annual return can grow to  ₹8.74 crore in 30 years. Check how compounding, understanding of core principles, disciplined investing and staying invested help create long-term wealth for retirement, children's education and financial independence.

items.title: These 9 penny stocks rallied up to 360% in 6 months; 4 turned multibaggers
items.summary: These stocks were identified using specific filters: a market capitalisation below Rs 1,000 crore, a share price under Rs 20, and a minimum average trading volume of 5 lakh shares.

items.title: F&O Talk: Nifty IT gaining strong momentum, says Sudeep Shah; outlines HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank strategy after Q1
items.summary: The Nifty IT index has rebounded nearly 13.5% from its July 1 low and gained around 5% this week, signalling improving momentum. Technical indicators, including the RSI, MACD and Relative Rotation Graph (RRG), point to strengthening bullish sentiment. The 29,600–29,650 zone is the key resistance, while 28,500–28,550 is expected to act as immediate support.

items.title: Sold house for  ₹1.10 crore, tax computed on  ₹1.96 crore? ITAT explains when Section 54 relief is still available
items.summary: The Pune ITAT has ruled that capital gains computed using the higher stamp duty value under Section 50C do not automatically deny Section 54 exemption. The tax benefit, however, is available only if the taxpayer satisfies all conditions for claiming the exemption.

items.title: Will Sensex, Nifty extend gains on Monday? Q1 earnings, global tech selloff among 6 factors to steer D-St this week
items.summary: Dalal Street ended the week on a strong note, with the Sensex and Nifty gaining over 1% on Friday. Investors will track six key market triggers this week, including the peak of the Q1 earnings season, the escalating Iran-US conflict, surging crude oil prices, the global tech selloff, rupee movement and FII flows. Analysts remain cautiously optimistic on market sentiment.

items.title: Health insurance: 63% policyholders unable to switch from their plan — Here's why and key things to keep in mind
items.summary: An analysis by CoverSure showed that besides under-penetration of health insurance, another growing problem for many policyholders is that coverage exists but as many as 63% are unable to switch plans.

items.title: South Korea to expand use of won-denominated bonds for foreign investors
items.summary: South Korea has announced plans to enhance global acceptance of its currency by allowing foreign financial institutions to borrow won. Additionally, these institutions will be permitted to utilize won-denominated bonds as collateral. Furthermore, the dollar-won market trading hours have been expanded to twenty-four hours, seeking to integrate South Korea’s currency into the wider international markets.

items.title: Salary slip shows TDS deducted, but Form 26AS doesn't? Here's how the ITAT ruling protected the taxpayer
items.summary: An ITAT ruling reinforces that salaried employees cannot be asked to pay tax twice if their employer deducts TDS but fails to deposit it. The decision strengthens taxpayer protection and serves as a timely reminder to verify TDS records before filing income tax returns.

items.title: Q1 results FY27 to US-Iran war, FII flows: Top five triggers that may dictate the Indian stock market this week
items.summary: Stock market today: Benchmark indices registered solid weekly gains, with the Nifty 50 rising about 0.53% to end at 24,334.30. While some of the global triggers continue this week, read on to know what are the fresh factors to watch out for this week.

items.summary: Vikram-1 is a four-stage launch vehicle designed to carry payloads of up to 350 kg to low Earth orbit (LEO), the region of space where many Earth observation, communication and scientific satellites operate.

items.summary: This insightful feature from YourStory tests and strengthens your business acumen! Here are 5 questions to kick off this 241st quiz. Ready?

items.title: A viral SIP myth is fooling investors. Here's why
items.summary: A viral post claims SIPs delivered just 6.7% over two decades. But the calculation is flawed—and the flat market it cites is precisely when SIPs quietly do their best work.

items.title: After a decade of falling behind, UTI AMC begins its turnaround under a new chief
items.summary: Subramaniam, who took charge in February ahead of the annual planning cycle, likens his career to football—having progressed from team captain as head of equities to manager as chief investment officer, and now to running the club as chief executive.

items.title: Bank holidays this week: SBI, HDFC, ICICI, PNB to remain closed for upto 3 days — RBI calendar here
items.summary: Bank holidays this week: Banks in India will be closed for upto three days this week, but digital platforms like UPI and banking apps will remain operational. Customers should note that in-branch services are suspended on Sundays and specific regional holidays.&nbsp;

items.title: NTA probe finds NEET OMR sheets 'completely AI-generated', tampered; legal action likely
items.summary: The National Testing Agency found one disputed NEET OMR sheet was AI-generated. Another OMR sheet submitted for scrutiny was discovered to have been tampered with. This follows allegations from students regarding discrepancies in their recorded responses and final scores. The NTA will pursue legal action against those submitting fake or manipulated OMR sheets. Around twenty lakh candidates appeared for the NEET examination this year.

items.title: If OpenAI becomes ‘Lehman Brothers of AI bubble’ – What happens to Microsoft, Nvidia, Oracle and Amazon?
items.summary: Ed Zitron claims that the AI boom is primarily driven by OpenAI, cautioning that its collapse could trigger an industry downturn. Despite heavy investments, concerns exist over profitability, particularly as OpenAI's revenue struggles to match its infrastructure costs.

items.title: NPS onboarding redesign, AI-powered pension advisory platform, NPS Swasthya—PFRDA Chair encourages ‘responsible’ AI use
items.summary: PFRDA Chairman S Ramann outlined an AI roadmap to redesign the National Pension Scheme's onboarding process. He also encouraged ‘responsible’ AI use for a pension advisory platform powered by the technology.

items.title: Foreign asset details for 2022-24 now available under AIS: What to do if you missed disclosing them in previous ITRs?
items.summary: Taxpayers can now view their foreign assets and income via the AIS on the income tax e-filing portal. The AIS currently includes data for 2022-2024, with 2025 data expected by late 2026. Here's what to do if you missed disclosing them in previous ITRs.

items.title: SpaceX short sellers pocket $5 billion in paper profits as stock wipes out $1 trillion in value
items.summary: SpaceX's IPO generated headlines, but short sellers have reaped $5 billion in paper profits amid a stock decline. The short interest surged from $4.5 billion to $25 billion, with analysts remaining optimistic overall despite recent volatility and a market value drop to $1.63 trillion.

items.title: From Daman’s first flight to ₹28,840-cr UDAN push: India’s regional air connectivity takes off
ITR filing 2026: FD interest is taxable — here's how to report it correctly
items.summary: FD interest income is fully taxable but tax implications may vary based on the chosen tax regime and taxpayer age. Some deductions may also be available, based on your age and income profile. Here's all you need to know.

items.title: Income-tax returns: Check penalty for missing deadline for filing ITR — Key things to know
items.summary: ITR: Taxpayers must file their income-tax returns for FY26 / AY27 by 31 August this year, extended from 30 June. Here's how much penalty you can be charged and deadline for delayed ITR filing during the tax year.

items.title: Meta's 'AI overbuild' finds a buyer as Anthropic eyes $10 billion computing power deal
items.summary: Meta is negotiating a deal to lease AI computing power to Anthropic, potentially worth $10 billion, transforming a previously seen liability into a revenue opportunity.&nbsp;

items.title: Shanghai Xingshu launches first constellation of its space-based computing project
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan launched its first space-computing constellation on Saturday. This project aims to deploy a total of one thousand satellites in orbit. Space-based computing processes data in orbit, reducing the need for Earth transmission. This launch moves China closer to commercial operation of its space-based computing network. Elon Musk's SpaceX is also advancing its own space-based computing initiatives.

items.title: Delhi world's cheapest for dates, buying properties among major cities; salary among lowest
items.summary: New Delhi offers the world's cheapest date and broadband internet services. Property prices in Delhi remain relatively low compared to global markets. However, the city's salaries are among the lowest recorded globally. Everyday expenses have significantly decreased in dollar terms over the past decade. Delhi's quality of life rankings did not place it among the top cities.

items.title: IPL 2026 drives JioStar to record quarter as Q1 revenue climbs 14% YoY
items.summary:
The Indian Premier League once again proved to be JioStar's biggest growth engine, helping the media giant post its strongest quarter since the merger and pushing revenue close to the $1.14 billion mark.

For the quarter ended June 30, 2026, JioStar reported revenue from operations of Rs 10,946 crore ($1.14 billion), up 14% year-on-year, while EBITDA from operations ju

items.title: US data center protests go national as backlash grows
items.summary: Opponents of AI data center expansion plan nationwide protests this Saturday. HumansFirst is coordinating demonstrations in at least 125 locations across the United States. Protesters voice concerns about environmental impact and community autonomy. A recent poll indicated low public support for local data center construction. This coordinated national effort highlights growing public opposition to AI infrastructure.

items.title: Lohia Corp IPO opens on July 23: Here’s all you need to know
items.summary: Lohia Corp will launch its IPO on July 23, with the issue comprising a pure offer for sale of over 2.59 crore shares. The technical textile machinery maker reported a 64% YoY jump in FY26 net profit and a 25% increase in revenue, ahead of its market debut.

items.title: JK Cement Q1 profit drops 15.3% YoY to Rs 274.62 crore
items.summary: JK Cement Ltd reported a 15.3 percent decline in net profit for the June quarter.  The company posted Rs 274.62 crore profit, down from Rs 324.25 crore last year.  Revenue from operations increased by 20.25 percent to Rs 4,031.72 crore.  Total expenses rose by 25.5 percent during the same period.  JKCL's total income, including other income, grew 19.41 percent.

items.title: Complaints on quality of seeds, fertilisers and pesticides to be probed, resolved in 7 days: Bharane
items.summary: Maharashtra's agriculture minister announced revamped taluka committees for faster complaint resolution. These committees will now investigate seed, fertiliser, and pesticide quality issues within seven days. Field inspections and sample testing will ensure impartial inquiries into farmer complaints. Representatives of companies and retailers must attend inspections and provide purchase receipts. Strict action will follow against manufacturers and dealers supplying substandard agricultural input

items.summary: Saturation doesn’t seem to be a concern that plagues India’s D2C boom. What began as a handful of digital-first brands…

items.summary: Yes Bank expects margins to improve through lower funding costs and remains open to a microfinance acquisition, though none is planned immediately.

items.title: RBL Bank Q1 Results: Net profit rises 27% YoY to Rs 254 crore; Emirates NBD ownership boosts growth outlook
items.summary: RBL Bank reported a 27% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 254 crore, while net interest income increased 12%. The lender said Emirates NBD’s majority ownership has expanded its growth opportunities, with management expecting stronger momentum in corporate banking, trade finance and cross-border business.

items.title: IDBI Bank Q1FY27 results today: Net profit up 5% to  ₹2,115 crore, business grows 15% YoY — Here's all we know
items.summary: IDBI Bank Q1FY27 results today: The bank declared its earnings for the quarter on 18 July with net profit up 5% to  ₹2,115 crore, net interest income risen by 10% YoY to  ₹3,486 crore, and business growth at 15% YoY at  ₹5.84 crore.

items.title: IDBI Bank Q1 Results: Net profit grows 5% YoY to Rs 2,115 crore, NII climbs 10%
items.summary: IDBI Bank reported a 5% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 2,115 crore, while net interest income grew over 10%. Deposits and advances posted healthy growth, asset quality improved year-on-year despite a marginal sequential uptick in NPAs, and capital adequacy remained robust.

items.title: Fred Kelly’s timeless investing lessons: Why the crowd is usually wrong
items.summary: Legendary investor Fred C. Kelly argued that investment success depends more on mastering psychology than predicting markets. In his classic work, he explains why following the crowd often leads to losses and how patience, discipline and independent thinking can help investors avoid costly emotional mistakes.

items.title: Indian Listed New-Age Tech Company Tracker: Market Cap, Revenue & More
items.summary: For years, we at Inc42 have tracked the Indian tech startup ecosystem and seen it grow from a kid to…

items.title: IPO GMP Check: SBI Funds Management IPO vs Alpine Texworld IPO - What grey market signals ahead of listings?
items.summary: SBI Funds Management and Alpine Texworld IPOs closed their bidding on July 16, with SBI commanding a GMP of  ₹91 and being subscribed 41.66 times. Alpine Texworld had a GMP of  ₹1 and was subscribed 1.40 times, indicating differing market performances ahead of their listings.

items.title: Gujarat tops NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index 2026
items.summary: Gujarat leads India's major states in NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index 2026. The state achieved the highest score, reflecting its investor-friendly business environment. Gujarat's policy stability and efficient single-window system attract significant investor confidence. World-class industrial infrastructure and strong logistics further enhance its competitive advantage. The state's robust economic fundamentals and fiscal discipline support sustained investment growth.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q1 results 2026: Net profit climbs 5% YoY to  ₹19,059 crore
items.summary: HDFC Bank reported a net profit of  ₹19,059 crore for Q1 FY 2026-27, a 5% YoY increase. Details here.

items.title: ICICI Bank Q1 Results: Profit jumps 16% YoY to Rs 14,805 crore; NII rises 13% as margins expand
items.summary: ICICI Bank Q1 Results:  ICICI Bank posted a 15.9% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 14,805 crore, while net interest income grew 12.7%. Asset quality improved, advances and deposits registered healthy growth, and provisions declined, supported by strong core operating performance and robust fee income during the quarter.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q1 Results: Net profit rises 5% YoY to Rs 19,060 crore, NII up 7%
items.summary: HDFC Bank Q1 Results: HDFC Bank reported a 5% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 19,060 crore, while net interest income increased 7%. Profit beat Nomura’s estimate but missed Kotak’s forecast. Asset quality remained stable, provisions declined sharply year-on-year, and the bank maintained a strong capital adequacy ratio.

items.title: ICICI Bank Q1 Results 2026: Net profit rises 16% to  ₹14,804.50 crore
items.summary: ICICI Bank reported a net profit of  ₹14,804.50 crore for Q1 2026, reflecting a 16% increase from  ₹12,768.21 crore in Q1 2025. The earnings announcement was made on July 18, 2026.

items.title: Govt explores linking land records with ULI for faster farm credit
items.summary: The Department of Land Resources is exploring digital land record integration with ULI. This initiative aims to provide faster and secure institutional credit access. Discussions with RBI Innovation Hub focused on leveraging digital public infrastructure for lending. The collaboration seeks to streamline credit delivery for farmers and rural citizens. This integration will enhance transparency and efficiency in the lending ecosystem.

items.title: Skyroot Aerospace joins small group of companies capable of orbital launches
items.summary: The mission, called Aagaman, marks the culmination of years of development for the startup, which had earlier flown the sub-orbital Vikram-S in 2022. Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated the Skyroot team shortly after the launch, calling it proof of India's push toward self-reliance in space technology.

items.title: Bitcoin rebounds to $64K after AI-led selloff triggers crypto rout. Here's what experts say
items.summary: Bitcoin recovered towards $64,000 after an AI-driven selloff pushed the cryptocurrency below the $63,000 mark. Experts said risk-off sentiment weighed on crypto markets, though softer US inflation data helped revive investor confidence. Analysts highlighted key technical levels and cautioned that the broader downtrend remains intact.

items.title: China's Kimi K3 rattles US AI industry
items.summary: Kimi K3, a new artificial intelligence program from a Chinese startup called Moonshot AI, stunned the US tech industry on Friday, setting off fresh discussion over the China-US rivalry to dominate AI. The program was released Thursday and within hours hit the top spot on a widely watched ranking of AI coding tools called Arena, marking the first time a Chinese model had claimed the number one position on the list.

items.title: China's DeepSeek to raise fresh capital at $74 billion valuation ahead of onshore IPO
items.summary: The plan comes just weeks after the ​Hangzhou-based company, which drew global attention with its low-cost AI models in 2025, raised about $7.4 billion in June at a post-money valuation of about 450 billion yuan, the people said. Filings by two Chinese investors later suggested DeepSeek was valued at 350.88 billion yuan, or around $52 billion.

items.title: Yes Bank Q1 results 2026: Net profit jumps 33.7% YoY, operating profit up 25.5%
items.summary: Yes Bank Q1 results 2026: During the April to June 2026 period, Yes Bank reported net profit of  ₹1,071 crore, up 33.7% Y-o-Y &amp; 0.2% Q-o-Q

items.title: Yes Bank Q1 Results: Net profit surges 34% YoY to Rs 1,071 crore; NII advances 18%
items.summary: Yes Bank Q1 Results:  Yes Bank reported a 34% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 1,071 crore, while net interest income grew 17.5%. Asset quality improved year-on-year but saw a sequential uptick in NPAs. The bank said stronger core earnings and steady margins reflect improving business fundamentals.

items.title: PNB Q1 Results: Net profit surges 214% YoY to Rs 5,253 crore; NII up 2%
items.summary: PNB Q1 Results: Sequentially,  however, net profit rose marginally by a little over 0.5% QoQ from Rs 5,225 crore reported in the preceding three months.

items.summary: FIIs and retail investors increased stakes in 88 stocks during Q1FY27. ETMarkets highlights 12 stocks that rallied up to 135% in six months, including five multibaggers.

items.title: Skyroot’s Vikram-1 lifts India’s private space ambitions into orbit
items.summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 successfully reached orbit, becoming India’s first privately developed orbital rocket. The milestone validates space-sector reforms, strengthens domestic launch capabilities, and signals India's emergence as a competitive commercial space power.

items.title: PM Modi congratulates Skyroot after private rocket reaches orbit: ‘You have planted new seeds in the sky’
items.summary: In a phone conversation following the launch, Modi told founder Pawan Chandana and the Skyroot team that they had “planted new seeds in the sky” and given strength to young Indians to dream bigger in technology and space.

items.title: Supreme Court Seeks Snapdeal’s Reply In Prescription Drug Sale Case
items.summary: The Supreme Court has sought responses from Snapdeal and its directors on Karnataka government’s plea challenging a Karnataka High Court…

items.title: India tightens Sri Lanka tax treaty with anti-abuse rule
items.summary: India has amended its tax treaty with Sri Lanka to introduce a Principal Purpose Test (PPT), allowing authorities to deny treaty benefits where tax advantage is a key objective of an arrangement. Effective for income from FY28, the change targets treaty shopping and aligns the DTAA with global standards.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q1 Results 2026: Net profit rises 22.5% to  ₹5,480 crore
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank reported a 22.5% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit, reaching  ₹5,480.46 crore in Q1 FY27. Total income for the quarter rose to  ₹30,068.60 crore, with standalone net profit at  ₹4,122.96 crore.

items.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q1 Results: Net profit climbs 26% YoY to Rs 4,123 crore; NII rises 9%
items.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q1 Results:  Kotak Mahindra Bank reported a 26% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 4,123 crore, while NII increased 9%. Asset quality improved year-on-year, with gross and net NPAs declining, though margins moderated during the quarter. The bank’s deposits and advances continued to grow.

items.title: States' fiscal deficit likely to moderate to 3.4% of GSDP in FY27 as tax collections improve: ICICI Bank
items.summary: States' fiscal deficit is expected to moderate to 3.4 per cent of Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) in FY27, supported by stronger tax collections and an improvement in revenue receipts, according to an ICICI Bank report.

items.title: Spacetech unicorn Skyroot launches Vikram-1, marks India's first private orbital rocket mission
items.summary: The mission, named Aagaman, lifted off from the First Launch Pad at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC-SHAR) in Sriharikota at 12:05 noon. The mission completed all four stages as planned. The success marks India's first privately developed orbital launch vehicle mission and a major leap towards building commercial, on-demand satellite launch services from the country.

items.title: Axis Bank Q1 Results 2026: Net profit jumps 23% YoY to  ₹7,114 crore; NII rises 8%
items.summary: Axis Bank reported strong Q1 FY27 earnings, with standalone net profit rising 22.5% to  ₹7,114 crore and net interest income growing over 8% to  ₹14,646 crore, surpassing Street estimates.

items.title: Skyroot Creates History With India’s First Private Orbital Rocket Launch
items.summary: Spacetech unicorn Skyroot created history by becoming the first private Indian company to place a rocket into orbit. Its Vikram-1…

items.title: MakeMyTrip IPO soon! Online travel giant files confidential DRHP with SEBI, 15 years after Nasdaq listing
items.summary: MakeMyTrip has initiated the process for its India IPO by filing a Draft Red Herring Prospectus with SEBI. The offering involves an equity share sale by MakeMyTrip Limited and its subsidiary, ibibo Group, but details like the issue size remain undisclosed.

items.title: Axis Bank Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 23% YoY to Rs 7,114 crore; NII up 8%
items.summary: Axis Bank Q1 Results: Axis Bank reported a 22.5% YoY rise in Q1 FY27 standalone net profit to Rs 7,114 crore, while net interest income increased over 8% to Rs 14,646 crore, beating estimates. Asset quality remained stable, with the lender reporting strong balance sheet growth, robust deposit mobilisation and healthy loan expansion.

items.summary: The Indian startup ecosystem continued to mature in FY26, with 22 new-age tech companies making their public market debut as…

items.title: The New Oil? Why the world is chasing copper
items.summary: Copper has emerged as a strategic commodity powering the global shift towards clean energy, electric vehicles and digital infrastructure. With demand rising faster than supply due to electrification and constrained mining output, the metal is increasingly viewed as a critical resource for the global economy's long-term transition.

items.title: Sony and Amitabh Bachchan announce Kaun Banega Crorepati Season 18
items.summary:
Sony Pictures Networks India (SPNI) and Amitabh Bachchan unveiled the theme of the new season of Kaun Banega Crorepati (KBC) – ‘Sochna Padega’.

‘Sochna Padega’ brings to spotlight - application of knowledge creates advantage. Kaun Banega Crorepati (KBC) Season 18 will premiere on Monday, August 10 at 9:00

items.title: Concurrent Gainers: 13 smallcap stocks gain for 5 days in a row
items.summary: Thirteen BSE SmallCap stocks rallied 10-15% after posting gains in each of the last five trading sessions, outperforming the broader market's modest advance.

items.title: Anthropic includes Fable 5 for top-tier subscriptions after weeks of extensions
items.summary: Under the new terms, Max and Team Premium subscribers will get Fable 5 built into their plans indefinitely, capped at 50% of their regular usage limits. Pro and Team Standard subscribers will not get the model included in their base plans, but will keep access through usage credits. The company is adding to that with a one-time $100 credit for affected users.

items.title: Agentic AI, alternative data and SIFs take centre stage at Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026
items.summary: Agentic AI, alternative data and Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) dominated discussions at the Indian Institutional Quant Conference 2026, where industry leaders, academics and policymakers explored the evolving role of quantitative investing, AI-driven strategies and regulatory developments shaping India's institutional investment landscape.

items.title: Onion growers welcome agri market near Vadhvan port, urge govt to develop Nashik as export hub
items.summary: Maharashtra's onion growers welcome a new agricultural market near Vadhvan port. They propose developing Nashik as a national onion export hub for global trade. This initiative aims to boost farmer incomes and India's position in the onion market. The growers seek direct farmer benefits and improved export infrastructure. A stable export policy and processing incentives are also requested.

items.title: Xtranet Technologies announces IPO dates for launch next week. Check details
items.summary: Xtranet Technologies will launch its Rs 166.8 crore IPO on July 23 at a price band of Rs 120-127 per share. The issue, comprising a fresh issue of 1.31 crore shares, will fund debt repayment, capital expenditure and working capital, with listing scheduled on July 30.

items.title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 20 July 2026
items.summary: Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking, highlighted that from a technical perspective, the index is now approaching the crucial resistance zone near 200-Day EMA, where a decisive breakout could trigger further upside momentum.

items.title: Longer-dated yields lower after data, on track for weekly decline
items.summary: Longer-dated U.S. Treasury yields declined on Friday after recent economic data. Markets largely priced out a Federal Reserve rate hike for this month. Import prices increased unexpectedly, while consumer and producer prices showed moderation. Geopolitical worries and hawkish Fed comments added to market uncertainty. Bond yields moved lower, suggesting a flight to safety amid rising oil prices.

items.title: Czech government cuts bond issuance plan after strong demand for retail bonds
items.summary: Czech Finance Ministry will reduce planned bond issuance by 130 billion crowns. This decision follows strong demand for retail bonds offered recently. Overall gross borrowing needs for 2026 remain largely unchanged. Two more retail bond offerings are scheduled for later this year. This comes after 74 billion crowns sold in the first round.

items.title: Oil settles up on renewed US-Iran hostilities and threat of Red Sea closure
items.summary: Oil prices climbed significantly on Friday, reaching their highest point in over a month. Escalating hostilities between the United States and Iran intensified attacks across the Gulf region. Shipping routes face potential closure, impacting global oil flows and prices. Saudi Arabia has redirected significant crude exports to avoid the Strait of Hormuz. Ukraine also reported striking a Russian oil refinery in its conflict.

items.title: Gold set for biggest weekly drop since early June on inflation, rate-hike worries
items.summary: Gold prices saw an increase on Friday, driven by escalating U.S.-Iran tensions. These geopolitical events fueled energy prices and inflation concerns, impacting market sentiment. Higher inflation fears reinforced expectations of potential U.S. interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve. While gold is a hedge against inflation, higher rates typically pressure the non-yielding metal. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also experienced weekly losses.

items.title: Tech selloff weighs on European shares ahead of ECB meeting next week
items.summary: European stocks edged lower Friday, influenced by a global tech sell-off and Middle East tensions. Investors await the European Central Bank meeting and corporate earnings reports next week. Rising oil prices and subdued reactions to strong results complicated the market backdrop. Tech stocks experienced a significant decline this week, impacting major AI beneficiaries. Utilities and defense sectors saw gains, while luxury brands faced headwinds from reduced tourist spending.

items.title: IPO calendar: 5 IPOs opening for subscription to keep investors busy; SBI Funds among 4 listings scheduled
items.summary: The IPO market will remain active next week with five public issues opening for subscription, including mainboard offerings from Xtranet Technologies and Cube Highways Trust InvIT. Investors will also watch four listings, led by SBI Funds Management, as strong demand and healthy grey market premiums keep primary market sentiment buoyant.

items.title: JSW Steel’s Q1 profit soars 2x YoY on robust topline growth
items.summary: JSW Steel's profit more than doubled year-on-year to 4,696 crore in the June quarter. Robust revenue growth and higher volumes aided the company's strong financial performance. Consolidated revenue from operations rose around ten percent on year to 47,364 crore. Total expenses grew less than four percent on year, which helped profitability. The company's net debt also saw a significant reduction during the period.

items.title: Skyroot Aerospace to undertake maiden orbital launch of Vikram-1: PM Modi
items.summary: "A historic new frontier for India's space journey! At 11.30 am today, Skyroot Aerospace will undertake the maiden orbital launch of Vikram-1, India's first privately developed launch vehicle," Modi said on X.

items.title: Wall Street's chip index enters bear market! Is the AI bubble finally going bust?
items.summary: Wall Street's semiconductor index slipped into bear market territory after tumbling more than 20% from its June peak, raising fresh questions over the sustainability of the AI-driven rally. A new AI model from China's Moonshot and concerns over heavy AI spending triggered a broad selloff across global technology stocks.

items.title: BSE, Sona BLW among 10 midcap stocks that rose up to 53% in 2026. Do you own any?
items.summary: Midcap stocks have staged a strong comeback in 2026, with Oracle Financial Services, Solar Industries and BSE delivering gains of up to 53% as market sentiment and earnings outlook improved.

items.title: Fairfax picks up half of 3-year government bonds at auction
items.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings likely purchased government bonds as a prelude to acquiring IDBI Bank.  This marks the company's second significant bond purchase in recent months.  The Canadian firm is reportedly preparing to invest capital for a potential IDBI Bank stake.  Fairfax aims to secure a major stake in the government-owned lender.  The IDBI Bank privatisation process has been ongoing since October 2022.

items.title: NIfty IT logs best weekly gains since Oct 2025
items.summary: Indian IT stocks experienced a significant rebound this week.  The Nifty IT index saw its best weekly gain since October 2025.  This rally followed a sell-off in Asian AI and semiconductor stocks.  First-quarter earnings from major IT firms showed minimal AI disruption.  Investor confidence improved, leading to some short-covering in the sector.

items.title: Reliance Retail's Q1 profit drops 14% YoY but revenue rises 8%
items.summary: Reliance Retail Ventures reported a fourteen percent profit decline last quarter. Revenue from operations grew eight point two percent year-on-year. Investments in e-commerce and digital infrastructure pressured operating margins. The company aims to double operating Ebitda over three years. Reliance Retail expanded its store network to over twenty thousand locations.

items.title: Stocks to buy under  ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
items.summary: Mehul Kothari from Anand Rathi suggests a cautious approach for traders, recommending a decisive close above 24,400 for a bullish outlook. For stocks under  ₹200, he recommends Suzlon Energy, IDBI, and Zee Entertainment with specified buy prices and targets.

items.title: D-Street bucks Asian markets' meltdown
items.summary: The NSE’s Nifty 50 rose 261.55 points, or 1.1%, to close at 24,334.3, while the BSE Sensex gained 964.58 points, or 1.25%, to end at 78,151.45.

items.title: From Emergent To Neo Group  — Indian Startups Raised $281 Mn This Week
items.summary: Indian startup funding rebounded sharply in the third week of July. Between July 11 and July 17, Indian startups cumulatively…

items.title: Investors take to gold dip as Mideast war rages on
items.summary: Gold prices are experiencing their steepest weekly decline in six weeks. This drop has encouraged investors to return to the yellow metal. Consumers are also returning to jewelry stores after recent price reductions. Investors are showing caution towards silver, reducing their exposure. Gold remains a preferred core portfolio asset for many investors.

items.title: RIL's Q1 operational revenue hits milestone with 25% YoY jump
items.summary: Reliance Industries saw a twenty-five percent profit drop in the June quarter.  Revenue from operations increased twenty-five percent, crossing three lakh crore rupees.  Reliance Retail's net profit declined fourteen percent while revenue grew eight percent.  The oil-to-chemicals segment revenue rose over thirty percent amid market volatility.  Jio Platforms reported its first profit decline due to 5G asset capitalization.

items.title: Axis Bank Q1 preview: Profit, NII to stay healthy; NIM likely to shrink due to deposit repricing, competitive funding
items.summary: As Axis Bank gears up to announce its Q1 results, analysts predict significant growth in net interest income and net profit. However, a potential decline in net interest margin due to competitive pressures and deposit repricing raises concerns.

items.title: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit seen up 3.1% YoY to  ₹13,164 crore; NIMs, asset quality may remain stable
items.summary: ICICI Bank Q1 results preview: ICICI Bank’s loan growth in the June quarter is expected to be healthy at 18.5% YoY, led by gold loans, corporate loans, personal loans and mortgages. Deposits are seen rising 15.2% YoY.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: Net profit likely to rise 6% YoY on strong loan growth, NIMs to remain flat
items.summary: HDFC Bank Q1 results preview: HDFC Bank Q1 results are expected to be mixed, with NIM likely to remain largely stable sequentially, while asset quality is expected to remain steady. Analysts anticipate healthy double-digit loan growth, supported by strong business momentum.

items.title: Q1 results 2026: HDFC Bank to ICICI Bank, Axis Bank among over 20 companies to declare Q1 earnings today; full list here
items.summary: On July 18, over 20 companies, primarily banks, will announce their Q1 FY27 earnings, providing crucial insights into India's banking sector. Key indicators include profit growth and asset quality, with strong focus on management commentary amid rising geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX in talks to supply the Pentagon with computing power: Report
items.summary: According to the report, SpaceX employees have discussed plans to compete more directly with neocloud firms ​such as CoreWeave by selling computing capacity ⁠to AI ‌customers at lower prices.

items.title: Chinese AI model takes US tech industry by surprise with abilities rivalling Claude, ChatGPT
items.summary: The newest Kimi K3 model from Beijing-based startup Moonshot, run by a Pink Floyd-loving entrepreneur who earned his doctorate in Pittsburgh, appears to be catching up to the best versions of Anthropic's Claude and OpenAI's ChatGPT.

items.title: Why Netflix’s AI push isn’t reviving the stock
items.summary: The streamer says that gen-AI workflows now have been used in about 300 of the company’s titles.

items.title: These three large-cap stocks look cheap. Here's why investors should look closer
items.summary: These three large-cap stocks trade at relatively low valuations versus peers, but investors should look beyond cheap P/E multiples before making a decision.

items.title: Art and design: How this Four Seasons hotel celebrates the creativity of Bengaluru
items.summary: In this photo essay, we showcase a range of artworks from The Four Seasons Hotel at Embassy One, Bengaluru along with curator insights.

items.title: Meta, Anthropic in talks for potential $10 billion compute lease deal
items.summary: Such a deal would help Meta diversify beyond advertising by generating ​revenue from its infrastructure and competing with neocloud firms such as CoreWeave and Nebius, ​as growing adoption of advanced AI tools boosts the need for computing capacity.

items.title:
From Lithuania to the world: Hostinger’s evolution; 
How LinkedIn India is driving global products
items.summary: Hostinger CMO Kristina Strimaite believes artificial intelligence will not take away jobs, but it will leave behind those who refuse to engage with it. Malai Lakshmanan, Head of India Engineering at LinkedIn, discusses the evolution of the Bengaluru centre.

items.title: It's a revamp! RBI steps ahead on plan for plastic rupee notes
items.summary: India's currency printing authority is now on the lookout for international suppliers of polymer banknote material, signaling a significant change from nearly a century of using paper notes. Bidders must adhere to strict security protocols and provide evidence of past collaborations with central banks. The Reserve Bank of India is considering polymer notes due to their durability, coinciding with a recent drop in currency printing costs.

items.title: Governance is the new moat in AI era: ServiceNow Top executive
items.summary: Kulmeet Bawa, managing director for India and SAARC at ServiceNow said the company’s next phase of growth lies in becoming the “AI control tower” for enterprises by orchestrating AI workflows across multiple software systems rather than competing directly with foundation model providers.

items.title: Tata Electronics cyberattack: Mandiant recommendations implemented to prevent future breaches
items.summary: On June 23 ransomware group World Leaks posted more than 200,000 files to the dark web, including details on projects related to some of the company’s global clients, which include Apple and Tesla. Three days later, it said that the company had launched a probe and had tightened internal security protocols.

items.title: Beaten down IT majors catch fancy of prop traders
items.summary: Selling cash shares borrowed from HNI clients , buying futures on IT stocks yields high single-digit gains for traders

items.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on Satya MicroCapital Limited
